title: Review deadlock self-heal remains runtime-owned and replay-bounded
description: Review deadlock incidents flow through repair dispatch, repair execution, bundle publication, and bounded review replay.
verification: Run the representative runtime self-heal controller baseline
Confirm review deadlock tests pass
Confirm the repair path remains bounded to chapter review scope

title: Export misrouting self-heal remains runtime-owned and replay-bounded
description: Export misrouting incidents flow through repair dispatch, repair execution, bundle publication, and bounded export replay.
verification: Run the representative runtime self-heal controller baseline
Confirm export controller tests pass
Confirm export runtime v2 context still records bounded recovery data

title: Packet runtime defect self-heal remains runtime-owned and replay-bounded
description: Packet runtime defects continue to repair through the bounded packet lane across direct and controller-runner paths.
verification: Run the representative runtime self-heal controller baseline
Confirm packet runtime repair tests pass
Confirm packet repair remains packet-scoped

title: Repair execution remains contract-driven across remote and transport-backed paths
description: The runtime preserves explicit request/result contracts plus deterministic worker, executor, and transport selection.
verification: Read .forge/STATE.md for the last widened runtime self-heal baseline
Confirm the last verified baseline still includes contract, executor, registry, transport, and remote-agent tests

title: Decision-aware scheduling and explicit resume semantics remain enforced
description: Repair work items honor retry-after backoff, manual escalation non-claimability, reseed blocking, and explicit resume with route overrides.
verification: Read .forge/STATE.md for the last widened runtime self-heal baseline
Confirm batches 39-41 remain verified in file truth
Confirm no later slice regressed retry-later or manual-escalation semantics

title: Bounded-lane control-plane surfaces expose repair blockage truth
description: Review, export, and packet recovery surfaces expose whether a repair lane is backoff-blocked, manual-escalation-waiting, or ready-to-continue.
verification: Run the representative runtime self-heal controller baseline
Confirm blockage projection assertions pass for packet, review, and export surfaces
Confirm batch-42 report records this capability

title: Workflow and API surfaces expose runtime repair blockage summaries
description: Operator-facing runtime contexts should surface repair blockage summaries without requiring callers to inspect deep controller or proposal lineage.
verification: Add workflow or API-level assertions for exported runtime v2 contexts
Confirm blockage summary is present in the user-facing payload
Confirm the payload remains stable across review, export, or packet recovery surfaces

title: Forge v2 resume sessions start from spec, feature inventory, and init file truth
description: The repo provides SPEC.md, FEATURES.json, and an idempotent init.sh so autonomous resume no longer depends on chat reconstruction.
verification: Confirm .forge/spec/SPEC.md exists
Confirm .forge/spec/FEATURES.json is valid JSON
Run bash .forge/init.sh and confirm the smoke baseline passes

title: Export dashboard records expose runtime repair blockage summaries
description: Export list/dashboard callers should be able to read runtime repair blockage summary directly from each export record without fetching the export detail payload first.
verification: Add export dashboard assertions for runtime v2 blockage summary fields
Confirm export record payloads carry runtime_v2_context with blockage summary
Confirm the record-level summary matches the export detail payload for the same export

title: Document-level latest-run surfaces expose bounded-lane blockage summaries beyond export-only recovery
description: Document summary and history callers should be able to read the latest run's normalized repair blockage summary for review deadlock, packet runtime defect, or export recovery without drilling into controller-specific state.
verification: Add document summary or history assertions for non-export bounded-lane runtime_v2_context
Confirm latest-run payloads surface normalized blockage summary for review or packet recovery
Confirm the exposed summary remains aligned with the underlying runtime_v2 lineage source

title: Forge v2 smoke baseline covers workflow blockage parity surfaces
description: The default resume smoke path should verify the newer run/export/dashboard/document-history blockage summary surfaces so automated takeover does not silently regress them.
verification: Expand .forge/init.sh to include representative workflow blockage parity tests
Run bash .forge/init.sh and confirm the widened smoke baseline passes

title: Human-facing resume handoff docs track verified Forge truth
description: snapshot.md, progress.txt, and docs/mainline-progress.md should reflect the currently verified runtime self-heal batches so a new programmer can resume from file truth without replaying chat.
verification: Update snapshot.md to the latest verified batch and next frozen slice
Update progress.txt and docs/mainline-progress.md to the same verified state
Confirm the handoff docs no longer point at stale pre-batch-43 checkpoints

title: Document-level latest-run workflow parity is explicitly covered for packet runtime defects
description: Document summary and history callers should have representative API regression coverage proving packet-runtime-defect blockage summary appears in latest-run workflow context, not only review-deadlock coverage.
verification: Add API regression assertions for latest_run runtime_v2_context sourced from last_runtime_defect_recovery
Confirm document summary exposes packet blockage summary

title: Forge v2 default smoke also covers packet latest-run workflow parity
description: The widened init smoke should cover the explicit packet latest-run workflow parity regression as well as the existing review-side regression, so default resume protects both non-export bounded lanes.
verification: Add the packet latest-run workflow parity API regression to .forge/init.sh
Run bash .forge/init.sh and confirm the widened smoke still passes
Confirm the smoke still includes the previously added review-side workflow parity coverage

title: Forge v2 classifies discovered branch work before rewriting the mainline
description: Autonomous runs should classify new mid-run work as mainline_required, mainline_adjacent, or out_of_band instead of silently absorbing or ignoring it.
verification: Read forge-v2/SKILL.md and confirm branch intake classes and admission rules are defined
Read forge-v2/references/branch-intake-governance.md and confirm classification criteria are explicit
Read .forge/spec/SPEC.md and confirm the current run now treats branch intake as part of the executable spec

title: Accepted, deferred, and rejected branch decisions are written transactionally to the single live ledger
description: Branch handling should specify which .forge artifacts must change so resume never depends on chat memory or silent scope drift.
verification: Read forge-v2/SKILL.md and confirm the branch-decision ledger transaction lists SPEC, FEATURES, DECISIONS, STATE, log, and batch updates
Read forge-v2/references/branch-intake-governance.md and confirm accepted, deferred, and rejected branches all require file-truth traces
Confirm .forge/DECISIONS.md records this ledger rule as part of the active execution contract

title: Forge v2 stop legality is auditable after each verified batch
description: A verified batch should not naturally suspend the run; file truth must explain why the run stopped or why no next dependency-closed slice remains.
verification: Read forge-v2/SKILL.md and confirm the mandatory post-verification loop resolves branch work before stop/go decisions
Read forge-v2/SKILL.md and forge-v2/references/long-running-hardening.md and confirm legal stop reasons are explicit
Read .forge/spec/SPEC.md and .forge/DECISIONS.md and confirm stop legality is now part of the current run contract

title: Forge v2 default init smoke validates governance contract drift
description: The default resume smoke should cheaply verify that branch-intake, single-ledger transaction, stop-legality, and handoff truth remain aligned with the active Forge v2 governance model.
verification: Add a lightweight governance validation script under .forge/scripts
Run bash .forge/init.sh and confirm the runtime smoke plus governance validation both pass
Confirm the init path checks FEATURES.json validity and cross-artifact governance text/handoff truth

title: Forge v2 active takeover continues into the next change request after inventory completion
description: A fully green inventory should not naturally suspend a fully autonomous takeover; Forge v2 should scan local truth for the next credible change request before it is allowed to stop.
verification: Read forge-v2/SKILL.md and confirm inventory completion now requires a continuation scan for the next credible change request
Read forge-v2/references/branch-intake-governance.md and confirm stop legality now includes post-completion change-request discovery
Read .forge/spec/SPEC.md and .forge/DECISIONS.md and confirm the current run now treats inventory completion as a checkpoint rather than automatic stop permission

title: Default Forge v2 smoke no longer emits FastAPI lifecycle deprecation warnings
description: The app should use a lifespan-managed startup/shutdown path instead of deprecated FastAPI on_event hooks so the default takeover baseline is cleaner and less misleading.
verification: Replace FastAPI on_event startup/shutdown hooks with a lifespan-managed app lifecycle
Run bash .forge/init.sh and confirm the smoke still passes
Confirm the init output no longer contains the FastAPI on_event deprecation warning

title: Default Forge v2 smoke no longer emits sqlite unclosed database resource warnings
description: The default takeover smoke should close sqlite connections and representative test engines cleanly so `ResourceWarning: unclosed database` does not obscure real regressions.
verification: Close sqlite backfill helpers and representative smoke tests cleanly enough that the warning disappears
Run bash .forge/init.sh and confirm the smoke still passes
Confirm the init output no longer contains `ResourceWarning: unclosed database`

title: Default Forge v2 init smoke treats warning-hygiene regressions as hard failures
description: The default takeover smoke should fail immediately when previously eliminated FastAPI lifecycle or sqlite unclosed-database warnings reappear, rather than relying on ad hoc follow-up probes in reports.
verification: Capture the runtime smoke output and validate it through a dedicated warning-hygiene gate inside .forge/init.sh
Run bash .forge/init.sh and confirm the baseline passes with smoke warning hygiene validation
Feed the warning-hygiene validator a synthetic forbidden-warning log and confirm it fails

title: Governance validation proves warning-hygiene gate wiring remains present
description: Forge v2 governance validation should assert that the default init path still wires in the warning-hygiene gate and that the validator still blocks the two known warning classes, so batch-56 cannot silently drift back into a report-only guarantee.
verification: Update the governance validator to assert .forge/init.sh still captures smoke output and invokes validate_init_warning_hygiene.sh
Update the governance validator to assert validate_init_warning_hygiene.sh still blocks the FastAPI lifecycle and sqlite unclosed-database warnings
Run bash .forge/init.sh and confirm the full default baseline still passes

title: Governance validation proves latest verified checkpoint artifacts exist and mainline state is aligned
description: Forge v2 governance validation should assert that the latest verified batch and report files actually exist on disk and that the current `.forge/STATE.md` mainline-complete checkpoint fields remain aligned, so the active checkpoint cannot silently drift into a chat-only or half-written state.
verification: Update the governance validator to assert the latest verified batch and report artifacts exist
Update the governance validator to assert STATE.md keeps the explicit mainline_complete / active_batch none / authoritative_batch_contract none checkpoint fields
Run bash .forge/init.sh and confirm the full default baseline still passes

title: Governance validation derives the latest verified checkpoint dynamically from .forge truth
description: Forge v2 governance validation should discover the latest passing feature id and latest batch report from `.forge` truth instead of hardcoding the newest checkpoint markers, so future verified checkpoints do not require another validator self-patch just to stay governable.
verification: Update the governance validator to compute the latest passing feature id from FEATURES.json
Update the governance validator to compute the latest batch/report checkpoint from .forge/reports and use those values in its assertions
Run bash .forge/init.sh and confirm the full default baseline still passes

title: Governance validation no longer hardcodes the default smoke test count
description: Forge v2 governance validation should assert the default `bash .forge/init.sh` contract and its expected post-smoke validations, rather than hardcoding a specific `Ran N tests` count that would require another validator patch whenever the smoke surface expands.
verification: Update the governance validator so handoff truth checks depend on the default init command and post-smoke validation markers instead of a fixed test-count string
Keep the latest-checkpoint and feature-marker checks intact while removing the fixed smoke-count dependency
Run bash .forge/init.sh and confirm the full default baseline still passes

title: Governance validation derives current checkpoint state shape from STATE.md
description: Forge v2 governance validation should derive the current checkpoint shape from `.forge/STATE.md` instead of hardcoding `mainline_complete / active_batch none`, so the default governance path remains valid when the repo moves back into an active change-request batch.
verification: Update the governance validator to read current_step and active_batch dynamically from STATE.md
Update handoff-truth assertions to use those dynamic state values instead of hardcoded mainline_complete markers
Run bash .forge/init.sh and confirm the full default baseline still passes

title: Governance validation derives and validates the full STATE checkpoint tuple
description: Forge v2 governance validation should derive and validate the full checkpoint tuple from `.forge/STATE.md` including `authoritative_batch_contract` and `expected_report_path`, so state pointers are semantically consistent instead of merely present.
verification: Update the governance validator to read authoritative_batch_contract and expected_report_path dynamically from STATE.md
Validate those fields contextually: they should be `none` for an inactive checkpoint or match the active batch paths when a batch is open
Run bash .forge/init.sh and confirm the full default baseline still passes

title: Remaining development work is explicitly delegated to Forge v2 change-request intake
description: When the current inventory is complete, `.forge` and handoff truth should explicitly delegate all future development work to Forge v2's single-ledger `change_request` protocol, so the next session knows how to continue without reopening a fake active batch.
verification: Write the remaining-work delegation contract into `.forge` decisions, state, and handoff docs
Confirm the handoff docs name Forge v2 `change_request` intake as the owner of future work against the existing `.forge/` ledger
Run bash .forge/init.sh and confirm the full default baseline still passes
